name: Montebello Housing Development Corp.
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor offering HUD-approved homebuyer education, financial literacy, and down payment assistance to low-income families across Southern California.
description_long:
Montebello Housing Development Corporation (MHDC) was established in 1991 by the Montebello City Council to address affordable housing shortages in the region. Incorporated as a public service nonprofit in November 1992, the organization gained independence from city administration by 1999 and has grown into a dedicated team of four full-time and two part-time employees serving Los Angeles, San Bernardino, Riverside, and Orange counties.

MHDC provides comprehensive, HUD-approved services focused on enabling low- and very low-income residents to achieve homeownership. Their core offerings include homebuyer education classes, financial literacy programs, pre-purchase counseling, mortgage default counseling, and assistance accessing down payment assistance programs. All educational and counseling services are provided free of charge to clients.

The organization distinguishes itself through its explicit focus on generational wealth building and community economic stability. Rather than simply offering loan products, MHDC emphasizes equipping families with knowledge and tools to transition from renting to ownership. The organization is led by President Robert Monzon and a board including Dr. Joseph Sandoval (CFO), Jaime Legaspi (Vice President), and Dr. Vanessa Montañez (Secretary), with Operations Manager Karla Paniagua coordinating day-to-day activities.

As a legitimate non-profit with HUD approval and a two-decade operational history, MHDC is well-positioned to serve first-time homebuyers seeking education and counseling. However, the organization's reach is geographically limited to Southern California counties, and as a small nonprofit with limited staff, service availability may be constrained during peak demand periods. Prospective clients should verify current program enrollment status and timelines before contacting.
pros: HUD-approved services ensure compliance with federal housing counseling standards
All financial literacy, homebuyer education, and counseling services are provided completely free of charge
Serves four-county region (Los Angeles, San Bernardino, Riverside, Orange) with geographic flexibility
Explicit down payment assistance program access—helps clients qualify for mortgages they couldn't otherwise obtain
Addresses mortgage default counseling, supporting borrowers facing payment difficulties
Established 30+ year history since 1991 demonstrates organizational stability and community roots
Leadership includes CFO with doctorate degree (Dr. Joseph Sandoval), suggesting financial expertise
cons: Limited staff of only 4 full-time employees may result in long wait times or delayed responses during high-demand periods
Geographically restricted to Southern California counties only—cannot serve clients outside this region
Website lacks specific information about down payment assistance amounts, eligibility requirements, or typical approval timelines
No online application process or virtual counseling options mentioned; appears to require in-person contact
No third-party accreditation visible on website (e.g., NFCC certification) beyond HUD approval claim
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Southern California seeking free pre-purchase education and counseling
Low- and very low-income families needing financial literacy instruction before applying for mortgages
Homeowners facing mortgage payment difficulties or default risk in Los Angeles, San Bernardino, Riverside, or Orange counties
Renters seeking to build wealth through homeownership with professional guidance on the transition process
diagnosis: MHDC is best for low- to moderate-income first-time homebuyers in Southern California seeking free, comprehensive education and counseling to prepare for mortgage qualification and homeownership. The primary caveat is that the organization's small staff and geographic limitation to four Southern California counties may restrict availability, and prospective clients should call ahead to confirm current program capacity and enrollment timelines.
services: Down payment assistance program navigation and access
Community volunteer opportunities in housing development
Economic development support for low-income families
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
